REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED. FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT FAILS TO ALLEGE FACTS SUFFICIENT TO SUPPORT A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF FIDUCIARY DUTY AS TO EITHER DEFENDANT. PLAINTIFF'S ATTEMPT TO INTRODUCE EXTRINSIC EVIDENCE AT THE DEMURRER STAGE VIA A DECLARATION IS NOT SUPPORTED BY ANY AUTHORITY. DECLARATION OF ZOE GALLAGHER IS STRICKEN. WHETHER THE COURT GRANTS LEAVE TO AMEND OR NOT DEPENDS ON WHETHER THE TRIAL REMAINS ON NOVEMBER 4, 2024. IF PARTIES OBTAIN A TRIAL CONTINUANCE IN DEPARTMENT 206, DEMURRER WILL BE SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. IF TRIAL REMAINS ON NOVEMBER 4, 2024 DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. IF TRIAL IS CONTINUED, PLAINTIFF MAY APPEAR EX-PARTE PROVIDING AN UPDATE REGARDING THE TRIAL DATE ON NOVEMBER 6, 2024 AND OBTAIN AN ORDER GRANTING LEAVE TO AMEND. PROPOSED ORDER SUBMITTED FOR REVIEW. JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: KENNETH B. HUNT; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-01-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Gianna Geil; Moscone Emblidge & Rubens LLP; 415-362-3599; attorney for Defendants and Cross-Complainants Mart Haitjema and Elaine Chow.

John S. Worden; Venable LLP; 415-653-3750; attorney for Plaintiffs George Chih-Lun Yu and Frances Ho, Trustees of the George Chih-Lun Yu and Frances Ho AB Living Trust dated June 14, 2004

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Mr. Worden contests the court's tentative decision. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED. First Amended Complaint fails to allege facts sufficient to support a cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty as to either Defendant. Plaintiff's attempt to introduce extrinsic evidence at the demurrer stage via a declaration is not supported by any authority. Declaration of Zoe Gallagher is stricken. Whether the Court grants leave to amend or not depends on whether the trial remains on November 4, 2024. If parties obtain a trial continuance in department 206, demurrer will be sustained with leave to amend. If trial remains on November 4, 2024 demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. If trial is continued, Plaintiff may appear ex-parte providing an update regarding the trial date on November 6, 2024 and obtain an order granting leave to amend.

Proposed Order submitted for review. Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)
